title: Building
description: Static definition for a building type. See game-design/06-building-stats-and-defenses.md and game-design/02-bases-and-buildings.md for the design rationale behind each field. Live per-instance state (currentHP, hex, ruinState) is NOT part of this schema — see BuildingInstance/Wall in game-design/07-data-architecture.md. HP regeneration is a GLOBAL rule, not a per-building field: every building/wall regenerates 5% of its current max HP per 5-second resource tick while out of combat (see game-design/06-building-stats-and-defenses.md) — there is no hpRegenRate property on this schema.

category.description: Determines whether the building needs player input (Production = queue a troop) or just runs on its own once built. "Core" is a single-instance category for HQ only — it doesn't attack, produce, or apply an aura; its only mechanical effects are the capture-on-zero-HP rule and gating every other building's max level at that base (see game-design/02-bases-and-buildings.md).

extends.description: Id of another building this one is a variant of, e.g. River Battery/Flame Turret/Cold Turret/Grenade Tower extending "turret". Inheritance is shallow and field-level: any of defensiveStats/nonProductionUpgrade/populationCost present on this file override the corresponding field on the base building wholesale (e.g. providing defensiveStats.canTarget replaces the whole canTarget array, it does not merge array entries); anything omitted here is inherited unchanged. Purely a data-authoring convenience to avoid restating identical stat blocks across Turret variants — has no other engine meaning.

isStandalone.description: True for Road/Bridge/Dock/Tower — placed anywhere by an Engineer, not tied to a base (never appears in any BaseDef.buildableBuildings list — see data/bases/schema.json). Standalone instances carry their own ownerId directly (no baseId to derive ownership from) and, like Walls, do NOT ruin on destruction — a standalone building at 0 HP is deleted permanently, freeing the hex/edge; rebuilding it there is a fresh build at full cost, not a ruin-rebuild. See game-design/07-data-architecture.md.

isFixed.description: True for pre-seeded-only buildings (HQ, Ice Spire) that can never be freshly built from a menu, even after capture — only rebuilt if ruined. They also cannot be demolished.

materials.description: Material variants this building supports, if multi-material (Tower, Wall, Dock, Bridge). Each material has its own base_stats/stat_growth/base_cost/cost_growth block, keyed under materialStats.

materialStats.description: Only present for multi-material buildings. Keyed by material name, each value shaped like the non-production upgrade model below.

populationCost.description: Population slots this building consumes when placed. 0 for House and Walls.

placementRequirement.description: Where this building is allowed to be placed, beyond the base's HQ-radius and two-adjacent-buildings rules. Two independent constraints, since a building's own hex terrain and an adjacency requirement to a *different* terrain are separate concerns (e.g. Port sits on Plains but needs an adjacent Water tile).

siteTerrain.description: Terrain the building's own hex must be. Plains is the global default (see game-design/01-map-and-terrain.md — it's the only generally buildable terrain) and most building files omit this property entirely rather than restate it. Named exceptions: Forest for Treehouse's buildings, or Road (which unblocks a Forest hex for Land Vehicles by sitting directly on it); Hill for Windy Peaks' buildings; River for Bridge, which sits directly on the River hex it unblocks for Infantry/Land Vehicles (Naval already passes through River tiles regardless — a Bridge doesn't block or interact with Naval at all, it only changes the Infantry/Land terrain-cost for that hex).

adjacentTerrainRequired.description: Optional — at least one neighboring hex must satisfy this. "Water" is a unified check satisfied by either an Ocean tile or a River tile (there is no separate "Coast" terrain and no separate River-adjacency requirement — see game-design/01-map-and-terrain.md). E.g. Port/Shipyard/Harbour require "Water"; a non-Treehouse base's Lumber Mill requires "Forest".

hp.description: Health pool at level 1 (non-production/non-multi-material buildings) or see levels/materialStats otherwise.

rebuildCost.description: Percent of original build cost required to rebuild from a ruin, same material (e.g. 50 = 50%). Not applicable to Walls or standalone buildings (Road/Bridge/Dock/Tower), which delete outright on destruction instead of ruining.

defensiveStats.description: Only present for Defensive-category buildings (Turret, Missile Launcher, Grenade Tower, Flame Turret, Cold Turret, River Battery, Tower, Landmine).

damageTypes.description: Same mechanism as troop.schema.json's damageTypes — e.g. Flame Turret (Firebase) carries ["Fire"].

detector.description: Reveals stealthed units within detectionRange (or full visionRange if detectionRange is omitted), ignoring their revealRange. Revealing means the stealthed unit becomes visible/targetable to ANY of the owning player's troops/buildings within that same local radius, not just to this building itself — matters for Tower, whose own combat stats can lose to a full squad even once it reveals them; the point is stripping stealth for a garrison/wall/other defenses to finish, not necessarily soloing the kill.

detectionRange.description: Optional override for detector's stealth-sight radius, when it should differ from this building's own visionRange. Defaults to visionRange if omitted. Used by Tower (short detectionRange despite a long visionRange, so it clears normal fog-of-war far but only spots stealthed units up close) — Landmine's detector-side visibility (being spotted, not spotting) uses stealth/revealRange instead, an unrelated pair of fields.

selfDestructOnTrigger.description: True for one-time trap buildings (Landmine): once this building deals damage, it is destroyed immediately (deletes outright, like a standalone building at 0 HP — no ruin) rather than persisting as a repeating attacker. attackSpeed is meaningless for these and should be omitted.

type.description: e.g. "freeze", "stun", "knockback", "emp". "stun" is a GLOBAL rule, not per-instance config: it always applies a fixed -30% move/attack-speed debuff once the lockout (duration) ends, lasting that same duration again — in addition to the full lockout itself. "knockback" is an instantaneous displacement (magnitude = hexes shoved back), not a timed lockout. "emp" is domain-conditional (Land: movement disabled but can still attack, for duration; Air: instant destroy, no duration; Infantry/Naval: no effect) and respects a target's empImmune flag (data/troops/schema.json). See game-design/05-troop-stat-schema.md's Status Effects section. Don't add fields here to configure the stun tail; author only duration/magnitude/chance for the lockout, the tail reuses duration and its -30%/-30% magnitude is fixed.

chance.description: Percent chance (0-100) the effect applies on a successful hit. Omitted/100 = always applies, e.g. Cold Turret's freeze. E.g. a stun effect might set this to 30 for a 30% chance per hit.

detector.description: True if this building reveals stealthed units within detectionRange (or full visionRange if detectionRange is omitted), ignoring their reveal_range. Revealing means the stealthed unit becomes visible/targetable to ANY of the owning player's troops/buildings within that same local radius, not just to this building itself — Radar Array has no attack of its own, so its detector flag only matters because it exposes the target to whatever else is nearby. This is the top-level equivalent of defensiveStats.detector, for non-Defensive buildings that need the flag — currently only Radar Array (Support). Defensive buildings (Turret, etc.) set detector under defensiveStats instead.

detectionRange.description: Top-level equivalent of defensiveStats.detectionRange, for non-Defensive detector buildings. Optional override for detector's stealth-sight radius when it should differ from visionRange. Defaults to visionRange if omitted — currently omitted by Radar Array, which detects at its full (long) visionRange.

stealth.description: Mirrors troop.schema.json's stealth — this building is invisible to enemy troops/buildings beyond revealRange unless the observer has detector:true (at which point it's seen at full visionRange, ignoring revealRange). Required alongside revealRange. Currently only Landmine uses this.

revealRange.description: Required if stealth is true. Distance at which a stealthed building becomes visible to a non-detector, same mechanism as troop.schema.json's revealRange.

auras.description: Only present for Support-category buildings (Hospital, Ice Spire, Radar Array) that project a passive effect rather than attacking/producing.

magnitude.description: Always a plain number — never a unit-suffixed string like "6/s" or "+25%" — so the engine never has to parse it. Unit is implicit from effect: heal_over_time and upkeep_reduction are flat amounts (hp/s, and food+fuel upkeep each respectively), slow/production_boost/damage_reduction/speed_boost/attack_speed_boost are signed percent (e.g. -20 = -20%, 25 = +25%).

productionUpgradeLevels.description: Only present for Production-category buildings (except Command Centre — see commanderProgression below). This is the SOLE source of truth for which troop(s) this building produces and at what level: an explicit per-level table, one row per level, ordered. The building's max level is derived as length(productionUpgradeLevels), never stored separately; the ordered troop list is derived as productionUpgradeLevels.map(l => l.unlocks) rather than kept as a separate troopList array, so there's no second copy that can drift out of sync. A troop id may legitimately appear in more than one building's table (e.g. Rifleman unlocks from both Capital's and Treehouse's Barracks).

hp.description: Absolute value at this level, not a delta.

unlocks.description: Troop id unlocked at this level.

commanderProgression.description: Only present for Command Centre — replaces productionUpgradeLevels/troopList entirely. Commanders unlock by TIER (all Commanders of a tier become trainable at once), not one troop per level, and Command Centre level also grows the player's maxCommanders cap (see game-design/07-data-architecture.md's Commander Cap section).

tierLevels.description: Explicit per-level table for levels 1-3 — each level unlocks a whole commanderTier at once (see data/troops/schema.json's commanderTier).

unlocksTier.description: All Commander troops with this commanderTier become trainable at this Command Centre once it reaches this level. Lower tiers already unlocked remain trainable.

commanderSlots.description: This Command Centre's contribution to the player's maxCommanders cap at this level.

hp.description: Absolute value at this level, not a delta.

postTierGrowth.description: Levels 4+: every tier is already unlocked, so further levels are pure stat/cap growth — no new troops.

commanderSlotsPerLevel.description: Additional maxCommanders contribution gained per level from level 4 onward.

nonProductionUpgrade.description: Only present for non-production, non-multi-material buildings (Farm, Quarry, Mine, Oil Rig, Hospital, House, defensive buildings). Uncapped, formula-based growth, bottlenecked only by HQ level.

growthRate.description: Structured per-level growth rate — replaces an earlier magic-string format (e.g. "+10%_per_level") that the engine would have had to parse out of prose. mode:"percent" compounds as base*(1+value/100)^(N-1); mode:"flat" adds value*(N-1).

value.description: e.g. mode:"percent", value:10 means +10% per level; mode:"flat", value:0 means no growth.

statGrowth.description: Per-stat growth rate per level, e.g. {hp: {mode: "percent", value: 10}, range: {mode: "flat", value: 0}}.

costGrowth.description: Upgrade cost growth per level, intentionally faster than stat growth, e.g. {mode: "percent", value: 25}.

addsTurretPerLevel.description: Wood Tower only — each level adds an independently-targeting turret instead of pure stat scaling.

defensiveStatsOverride.description: Multi-material Defensive buildings only (currently just Tower). Some materials fight differently, not just at different numbers — e.g. Steel Tower loses Air targeting and gains splash, unlike Stone/Wood Tower which just scale damage/hp. Merged key-by-key on top of the building's shared defensiveStats block for this material only; omitted keys inherit the shared defensiveStats value unchanged. This is deliberately NOT the same semantics as the file-level `extends` override (which replaces whole blocks wholesale) — here individual keys merge so a material only needs to restate what actually differs.

minPopulationPerLevel.description: Only meaningful for HQ: a multiplier used to compute the population gate for upgrading HQ to level N, on top of the resource cost: requiredPopulationUsed = minPopulationPerLevel * (N - 1). E.g. value 3 means reaching level 2 requires populationUsed >= 3, level 3 requires >= 6, level 4 requires >= 9, and so on. Steep cost curve + this population gate is what paces HQ upgrades (see game-design/06-building-stats-and-defenses.md).
